Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Deposition Questions And For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Paul D. Herbert, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Payne & Fears, LLP
Joshua Rosenthal, Atty. for Defendant and opposing party: CMG Mortgage Services, Inc.

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Payne & Fears, LLP's Motion To Compel Answers To Deposition Questions is moot in part and granted in part. The motion is moot as to Numbers 1-7 in the separate statement as Defendant does not oppose the motion as to those questions.
The motion is granted as to the remaining questions. The court finds that there has been a waiver of any attorney-client privilege as to all matters regarding the representation of Defendant in the Wymac Actions. The analysis advanced by the parties treats the communications as being between counsel who acted separately. In fact Defendant has not disputed Plaintiff's contention that they were acting as co-counsel. By necessity, co-counsel share otherwise privileged matters as they are working in concert in the interests of the client. There is no privilege that exists between them that bars discovery of matters arising out of that joint representation. Defendant has not disputed Plaintiff's showing that the two firms shared the complete files. The two firms stand in each other's shoes, and any privilege is waived as to matters arising out of any communications had in that representation between the firms and between the firms and the client.
Defendant's Request To Present Oral Testimony At Hearing is denied.
Order was signed in open court.
COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. Reporter: Dorothy B. Darby, CSR # 11066
